findings.reason: Changes the default provider-resolution behavior of the canonical flow (runtime behavior); correct as substantive.
The central regression fix — threads live quota into the canonical dispatch path, directly affecting runtime wave sizing.

Adjusted from context_dependent: the removal verb falsely fired — probe.ts is @deprecated/unreferenced dead code and the queryLimits impls are additive no-ops; medium (not low) confidence. It meaningfully affects the quota/provider surface.
Centralizes the quota cascade and activates the provider-based resolveLimits rung; affects runtime limit resolution.
Adds new providers and shared-type/classification changes; additive but real new runtime code paths.
Correctness bug fix in path construction whose site is not yet located (medium confidence); needs careful location + Windows-aware testing.
Docs reconciliation + an `export` + a sync test; unambiguously good regardless of context.
Introduces a canary phase that gates the dispatch fan-out — changes dispatch runtime behavior (config-disablable).
Adds a sampling/coverage-budget mode plus partial-coverage reporting; affects dispatch behavior and synthesis output.
Computes and surfaces a fan-out confirmation signal consumed by the loader; affects dispatch metadata and host gating behavior.

Adjusted from context_dependent: the replace verb falsely fired — no behavior is removed. Its correctness depends on guaranteeing the bin is on PATH (O1 says it was not), so it warrants substantive review rather than a context-dependent label.
Adds scope-resolution mis-scope detection plus a loader confirmation gate; new runtime detection affecting host behavior.

Adjusted from safe: a design_review reading budget changes what the step reads and thus what the audit can find — a real (if low-severity) behavior change with prompt-rendering + config logic, not mere cosmetics.
Adds a schema pointer + a validation test against an already-existing schema; improves feedback timing without changing what the audit finds.
